<insights>

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
- Perfect cohesion (1.0) and zero coupling make this the simplest, most portable zone in the web package.
- Three-file footprint signals deliberate minimalism; changes here are safe and isolated from all other zones.
- Absence of entrypoints in the import graph suggests the landing page is served as a static asset rather than being imported by other modules.
- Zero coupling confirms this landing page can be updated, replaced, or removed with no ripple effects on the rest of the web package.
- The landing page and viewer each have their own index.html (web-3); verify the build pipeline correctly routes each HTML entry to its intended output path to avoid asset collisions.
- The landing page zone and the main web-dashboard-mcp-server zone have zero imports in either direction, confirming they are parallel, independently deployable surfaces within the same package — a clean separation that should be preserved as the web package grows.
- Landing page and viewer are fully decoupled parallel surfaces with no import path in either direction — this is an intentional, clean architectural boundary that allows each surface to be rewritten or replaced independently.
- landing.ts is 315 lines and 10.4 KB with zero inbound and zero outbound TypeScript import edges — confirmed by import graph. At this size with no module relationships, it is not a utility but a self-contained page bootstrap script loaded directly by the build tool via landing/index.html. The 'utility' archetype classification is incorrect and masks its role as an isolated entrypoint.
- landing.ts is classified as 'utility' but is a 315-line page entrypoint with zero module relationships in either direction. Misclassification as a utility causes it to be excluded from entrypoint audits (bundle size, CSP scope, initialization ordering), which are the relevant review criteria for a file of this type and size.
- The 'web-' prefix on this zone is consistent with other web-package zones, but the suffix '-landing-page' is a content descriptor rather than an architectural-role descriptor. Two other web zones use technology-plus-role suffixes ('-dashboard-mcp-server'), one uses a concern suffix ('-landing-page'), and three use positional identifiers (web-4, web-5, web-6 in the previous analysis snapshot). The mixed convention means web-* zones cannot be classified by prefix pattern alone — each zone name encodes a different classification axis.
- landing.ts is 315 lines, functions as a standalone page entrypoint, and has zero import relationships in either direction. Use the set_file_archetype tool to override its classification from 'utility' to 'entrypoint' so it appears in entrypoint audits (bundle size, CSP scope, initialization order). No code change is required — only the archetype metadata needs updating.
- [call graph] 10 internal calls, 0 outgoing, 0 incoming (cohesion: 1, coupling: 0)

</insights>
